accurate.why: Correctly identifies reauthorization through FY2029, CDC administration of the surveillance program, HHS administration of education/intervention activities, and IACC.

no_hallucinations.why: CDC as administrator of the Developmental Disabilities Surveillance and Research Program is accurate real-world context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as reauthorizing autism education, research, and resources programs.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Identifies the three programs reauthorized through FY2029 and the reporting requirement updates.

exceptions_conditions.why: High-level summary appropriately omits granular conditions; no material exceptions to note at this level.

major_provisions_covered.why: Omits the NIH activities expansion (Section 2), the appropriations increases, the communication tools section, and the youth transition report; these are significant operative provisions.

correct_entities.why: CDC, HHS, and IACC correctly identified as administrators.

correct_figures.why: No specific dollar amounts or dates beyond FY2029 stated.
